message.content: MLX is Apple's open-source machine learning framework designed specifically for Apple Silicon, enabling researchers and developers to run and train models directly on M-series chips using unified memory. Unlike frameworks such as PyTorch that were designed with NVIDIA GPUs in mind, MLX uses a lazy evaluation model and supports both CPU and GPU execution through Metal without requiring data copies between separate memory pools. The framework is built around a NumPy-like API that makes it approachable for researchers familiar with Python-based ML tooling, while its just-in-time compilation and function transformation system allows for efficient gradient computation. As of early 2026, MLX has grown a substantial ecosystem of model implementations including large language models, diffusion models, and audio transcription systems that are optimized to run at near-native speeds on MacBook Pro and Mac Studio hardware.

search_results.title: MLX: Apple's New ML Framework
search_results.snippet: MLX is an array framework for machine learning research on Apple silicon.

search_results.title: Getting Started with MLX
search_results.snippet: Learn how to build and train models using MLX on Apple Silicon Macs.
